channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — David Johansen, the wiry, gravelly-voiced singer and last surviving member of the glam and protopunk band the New York Dolls who later performed as his campy, pompadoured alter ego, Buster Poindexter, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida Man Games return for Round 2 as teams vie to be the world’s worst superhero
item.description: ELKTON, Fla. (AP) — The Florida Man Games are back for Round 2, as teams from around the state gather to prove they have what it takes to join the ranks of the world’s worst superhero. Following the success of last year's inaugural Florida Man Games in downtown St.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL KOZIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: No. 4 Houston clinches Big 12 regular-season title with 73-64 win over Cincinnati
item.description: L.J. Cryer scored 20 points and No. 4 Houston clinched the outright Big 12 regular-season title for a second straight season with a 73-64 win over Cincinnati on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, ABC 4

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike and engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oscar presenters Emma Stone, Andrew Garfield, Whoopi Goldberg rehearse before the show
item.description: Emma Stone knows her way around the Dolby Theatre, but the even the two-time Oscar winner needs a little rehearsing before the big show. This year she’s not a nominee but a presenter, though which category she’ll be announcing is under wraps until it’s broadcast live around the world on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah cheerleaders among evacuees after 'incident' at national competition in Texas
item.description: Utah cheerleaders and their family members were among the people evacuated from a Dallas, Texas, convention center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: For Gene Hackman, Santa Fe was a refuge from the limelight
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The piñon- and juniper-scented Rocky Mountain foothills overlooking Santa Fe have helped the city earn a reputation as a refuge for famous actors and authors seeking to escape the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Renowned geneticist Francis Collins retires from NIH, urging 'respect' for embattled workers
item.description: Dr. Francis Collins, a renowned geneticist, and former longtime director of the National Institutes of Health, is retiring from the government agency and urging “respect” for its embattled workers.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone dies in car crash
item.description: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone, a member of the all-female hip-hop trio The Sequence and known for the hit song “Wish I Didn’t Miss You,” was killed early Saturday in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after performance: Report
item.description: TMZ says per Stone's representative, Stone left a performance in Alabama early Saturday when she was killed in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Border czar makes the city of Boston his latest immigration target
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As Mayor Michelle Wu prepares to go to Congress to defend Boston's protections of immigrant communities, the city is in a war of words with the Trump administration and Republican representatives over how much police should support deportations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect attempts suicide as slain brother laid to rest: report
item.description: A New Jersey man who is accused of sadistically killing his brother and trying to eat his eyeball attempted suicide inside Thursday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown in New York.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a long-standing legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries where there's war or political instability to enter and temporarily live in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Flames from FedEx flight after bird strike near Newark Airport
item.description: A FedEx flight was forced to make an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a bird strike on Saturday morning, according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: School bus caught on camera engulfed in flames after driver's ‘quick response’ saves students from inferno
item.description: An Ohio school bus driver was credited with saving 15 middle school students as a bus went up in flames. The inferno was caught on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in Julio Foolio murder fights solitary confinement as prosecutors warns of witness threats
item.description: One of the men accused in the Julio Foolio murder case was back in court Thursday, this time focusing on his conditions in solitary confinement.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KRON4
